# gs run precommit-checks with .gitspice/precommit.yaml:
# happy path (single check succeeds) and
# fail-fast path (first check fails, second check is skipped).

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-05-04T12:00:00Z'

cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

git checkout -b feature
gs branch track --base main

# Happy path: a single check that succeeds.
mkdir .gitspice
cp $WORK/config/single-ok.yaml .gitspice/precommit.yaml

gs run precommit-checks
stderr 'echo-ok'

# Commit so the branch is non-empty.
git add .gitspice/precommit.yaml
gs cc -m 'test: yaml happy path'

# Failure path: two checks, first fails with fail_fast:true.
# The second check must NOT run.
cp $WORK/config/fail-fast.yaml .gitspice/precommit.yaml
git add .gitspice/precommit.yaml
gs cc -m 'test: yaml fail-fast'

! gs run precommit-checks
! stderr 'should-not-run'

-- repo/.keep --
-- config/single-ok.yaml --
checks:
  - name: echo-check
    cmd: echo echo-ok
-- config/fail-fast.yaml --
checks:
  - name: always-fail
    cmd: 'false'
    fail_fast: true
  - name: should-skip
    cmd: echo should-not-run
